components.title: DNS Server
components.description: Internal authoritative and recursive DNS service (Microsoft DNS integrated with Active Directory, or BIND) providing name resolution for internal systems and controlled recursive resolution to the internet via a filtering forwarder. DNS query logging is enabled and forwarded to the SIEM to support detection of DNS tunnelling, DGA (domain generation algorithm) beaconing, and lookups of known-malicious domains.
components.purpose: Provide reliable internal name resolution for organisational systems and controlled, logged, filtered recursive DNS resolution to the internet.

props.name: service-role
props.value: Authoritative for internal zones; recursive resolver forwarding to a filtering upstream service

props.name: redundancy
props.value: Minimum two DNS servers per site, AD-integrated zone replication

props.name: dns-filtering
props.value: Recursive queries forwarded to a DNS security/filtering service blocking known-malicious and newly-observed domains

props.name: logging
props.value: Query logging enabled, forwarded to SIEM for DNS tunnelling and DGA beaconing detection

protocols.name: dns
protocols.title: DNS — Name resolution (client queries and zone transfers)

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — DNS-over-HTTPS forwarding to filtering upstream (where used)

protocols.name: ssh
protocols.title: SSH — CLI administration (BIND) or RDP equivalent for Windows DNS

protocols.name: syslog
protocols.title: Syslog — Query and audit log forwarding to SIEM

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for the DNS Server.

implemented-requirements.description: An approved hardening configuration is maintained for DNS servers, covering: restricting zone transfers to authorised secondary servers only, disabling recursion on authoritative-only servers, restricting the administration interface to the management network, and keeping DNS server software current with vendor security updates.

DNS query logs and security-relevant events (zone transfer attempts, configuration changes, failed administrative logons) are centrally logged and forwarded to the SIEM, supporting detection of DNS tunnelling and domain generation algorithm (DGA) beaconing.

Patch management processes ensure the DNS server software (Microsoft DNS, BIND, or equivalent) is kept current with vendor security patches, tracked through the organisation's patch management schedule.
DNS administrators are assigned dedicated privileged accounts, separate from their standard user accounts, used solely for DNS zone and configuration administration.
Multi-factor authentication is required for administrators authenticating to the DNS management interface or the underlying server operating system.

Recursive DNS resolution to the internet is restricted to a controlled, filtered path (via a DNS security/filtering service or web proxy), rather than allowing internal hosts to query arbitrary internet DNS servers directly, reducing the risk of DNS-based data exfiltration and command-and-control.
